nativeRange.habitat: Reef crests and slopes with rich coral growth; tends to associate with sponges and tunicates at depths of 1–15 m

diet.notes: Primarily grazes on microalgae and vegetable matter; has been documented accepting frozen and dried foods containing marine algae and spirulina. Tends to pick at algae growth on live rock and tank surfaces throughout the day.

avoidWith: other Ecsenius blennies unless tank is large

summary: A small, charming reef blenny from the Philippines, generally considered beginner-friendly and reef-safe. Tends to perch on rocks and graze on algae; has two distinctive dark spots near the belly.
careNotes: The Two Spot Bimaculatus Blenny (also sold as Bimaculatus Blenny or Two Spot Blenny) is a small combtooth blenny native to the Western Pacific, specifically the Philippines and northeast Borneo (Sabah), Malaysia. FishBase records a maximum TL of 4.5 cm. It is generally observed perching on and around rocks, sponges, and tunicates on reef crests and slopes at depths of 1–15 m. In aquariums it tends to occupy the bottom and lower rockwork zones, grazing on microalgae throughout the day. A tight-fitting lid is strongly advised, as blennies of this group have been known to jump from open-top tanks. It tends to be territorial toward conspecifics and other Ecsenius blennies, particularly in smaller systems; one specimen per tank is generally recommended unless the aquarium is large enough to provide separate territories. It is considered reef-safe and has been kept successfully with corals and invertebrates. Care level is reported as easy by LiveAquaria and intermediate by BlueZoo Aquatics; as an algae grazer that readily accepts prepared foods, beginner is considered an appropriate classification. Temperature disagreement noted: LiveAquaria recommends 22–26°C (aquarium range) while FishBase wild-habitat data indicates 28.5–29.2°C; values used here are averaged across both sources (24–27.5°C). Breeding in captivity has been documented as oviparous with distinct pairing and demersal adhesive eggs, but is considered rare in hobbyist settings.
breedingNotes: Oviparous with distinct pairing; eggs are demersal and adhesive, attached to substrate. Captive breeding is rarely achieved in hobbyist aquariums.

additional.name: BlueZoo Aquatics
additional.notes: Cross-checked size (2 in vs LiveAquaria 1.5 in), tank size (24 gal vs 10 gal), and care level (intermediate vs easy). Averaged adult size; biased toward larger tank recommendation per playbook.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Used for authoritative size (4.5 cm TL), native range (Western Pacific: Philippines and NE Borneo/Sabah, Malaysia), taxonomy (Blenniidae, Blenniiformes), temperature (28.5–29.2°C wild preference), trophic level 2.0 (herbivore/detritivore), and breeding strategy.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous with distinct pairing; demersal adhesive eggs. Rarely bred in captivity by hobbyists.
